# Step 8 — Delete PR Confirmation Dialog

> A destructive confirmation dialog triggered when the Creator clicks **Delete** on a Draft PR. Confirming permanently deletes the PR and all its items with no recovery option. The Delete button is only available on **Draft** status PRs.

---

## 8.1 Access Path

- PR Detail (Draft) or Edit PR form → **Delete** button (red, visible only on DRAFT status)
- The Delete button is **not visible** on PRs in any other status

---

## 8.2 Flow Position

```mermaid
graph TD
    list["1. PR List"] --> create["2. Create PR"]
    list --> tmpl["3. Create from Template"]
    create --> detail["4. PR Detail"]
    tmpl --> detail
    detail -->|Edit| edit["5. Edit PR"]
    edit --> submit["6. Submit Confirmation"]
    submit --> inprog(["Submitted - In Progress"])
    inprog -->|Returned| returned["7. Returned PR"]
    returned --> edit
    detail -->|Delete| del["8. Delete Confirmation"]
    del --> list
    style del fill:#1a56db,color:#fff,stroke:#1a56db
```

---

## 8.3 Dialog Content

**Delete button in action bar (Draft PR):**

![Delete button in Draft PR action bar](data:image/png;base64,)

**Inline item row deletion reference** (row-level delete, not PR delete — shown for dialog pattern comparison):

![Edit form after inline item row deletion](data:image/png;base64,)

**Delete confirmation dialog (full viewport):**

![Delete PR dialog — full viewport](data:image/png;base64,)

**Delete confirmation dialog (focused):**

![Delete PR dialog — focused](data:image/png;base64,)

| Element | Confirmed Content |
|---------|------------------|
| Dialog title | **Delete Purchase Request** |
| Body message | Are you sure you want to delete "[PR number]"? This action cannot be undone. |
| PR reference | PR short-ID shown in quotes in the body message |
| **Delete** button | Red/Destructive — labelled **Delete** |
| **Cancel** button | Outline/Grey — labelled **Cancel** |

---

## 8.4 Delete Button Visibility Rules

| PR Status | Delete Button Visible? | Notes |
|-----------|----------------------|-------|
| `DRAFT` | ✅ Yes — Red button in action bar | Only available status |
| `INPROGRESS` | ❌ No | Creator can only Cancel (not Delete) in-progress PRs |
| `RETURNED` | ❌ No | Creator can edit and resubmit; not delete |
| `APPROVED` | ❌ No | Read-only |
| `COMPLETED` | ❌ No | Read-only |
| `VOID` | ❌ No | Read-only (per UI; BRD is ambiguous) |

> ⚠️ **Discrepancy:** BRD FR-PR-005A distinguishes between **Delete** (Draft only) and **Cancel** (INPROGRESS). The Cancel action for INPROGRESS PRs was not observed in the Creator view during capture and requires separate verification.

---

## 8.5 Validation on Action

### On Delete (button click)

**Purpose / Use Case:** Request deletion of a Draft PR — use when the PR was created by mistake or is no longer needed.

| # | Check | BR | Pass Behaviour | Fail Behaviour |
|---|-------|----|----------------|----------------|
| 1 | PR status is DRAFT | BR-01 | Delete confirmation dialog shown | Delete button not visible for non-Draft PRs |
| 2 | Creator is PR owner (Requestor) | FR-PR-005A | Delete proceeds on confirm | TBC — should reject if different user |
| 3 | No active PO linked to this PR | FR-PR-010 | Delete proceeds | TBC — if a PO was created, delete may be blocked |

### On Confirm (in dialog)

**Purpose / Use Case:** Confirm permanent deletion after reviewing the warning — this cannot be undone and removes the PR and all its items.

| # | Check | BR | Pass Behaviour | Fail Behaviour |
|---|-------|----|----------------|----------------|
| 1 | Server confirms PR still in DRAFT | FR-PR-006 | PR deleted; redirect to PR List | TBC — error if status changed by concurrent action |
| 2 | All item rows deleted | BR-01 | Items permanently removed | — |
| 3 | Budget commitment released (if any) | FR-PR-004 | Budget commitment reversal recorded | TBC |

---

## 8.6 Business Rules

| # | Rule | Source |
|---|------|--------|
| BR-01 | Creator can only **Delete** Draft PRs. PRs in any other status cannot be deleted by the Creator. | FR-PR-005A |
| BR-02 | Delete is **permanent and irreversible**. No trash / recycle bin. No undo. | FR-PR-005A |
| BR-03 | Deletion removes the PR and all associated items. The PR Number is retired (not reused). | FR-PR-001 |
| BR-04 | If a soft budget commitment was created (e.g. PR was submitted and returned, then edited to Draft), deletion releases the budget commitment. | FR-PR-004 |
| BR-05 | Only the Creator (Requestor) who owns the PR can delete it. Other users cannot delete another user's Draft. | FR-PR-005A |
| BR-06 | Delete action is logged in the audit trail: PR number, timestamp, user. | FR-PR-010 |
| BR-07 | Creator of an INPROGRESS PR can **Cancel** (not Delete) — requires a cancellation reason. Cancel is a separate action from Delete. | FR-PR-005A |

> ⚠️ **Discrepancy (BR-07):** Cancel action for INPROGRESS PRs was not observed in the Creator view. The "Cancel" button in the Creator's Draft edit form opens a "Discard changes?" dialog (navigation cancel), which is different from cancelling the PR itself. Needs verification with a test PR in INPROGRESS status.

---

## 8.7 Edge Cases & Error States

| # | Scenario | System Behaviour |
|---|----------|-----------------|
| 1 | Delete clicked — PR already INPROGRESS (race condition) | Server rejects; likely shows error toast or permission denied |
| 2 | Delete confirmed — network error | TBC: PR may or may not be deleted; retry guidance unclear |
| 3 | Delete confirmed — PR has linked PO (future state) | TBC: likely blocked with error message |
| 4 | Creator tries to delete another user's Draft | Delete button not shown (view only for other users' PRs) |
| 5 | Cancel button in dialog clicked | Dialog closes; PR unchanged; returns to Edit PR form |
| 6 | Escape key pressed in dialog | Dialog closes; PR unchanged (standard Radix Dialog behaviour) |

---

## 8.8 Navigation

| Action | Destination |
|--------|-------------|
| **Cancel** (dialog) | Step 5 — Edit PR Form (Draft, unchanged) |
| **Confirm Delete** (dialog) | Step 1 — PR List (deleted PR no longer visible) |
